channel.title: Mend.io Unified Release Notes
channel.description: Aggregated RSS, Atom, and HTML of all Mend release notes.

item.title: Mend for Bitbucket DC: Version 26.3.1 (23-March-2026)
item.description:
Unified Agent 26.2.2 | Renovate 43.59.4 | Remediate 26.3.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • (SCA) Added support for the uv package manager, enabling security scanning and Reachability analysis for Python projects managed with uv. Refer to this table for more details.

    • Note: The SCA orchestrator scanner environment variable must be enabled for uv detection to work (MEND_SCA_ORCHESTRATOR_ENABLED=true).

Resolved Issues

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where scans of .NET projects would fail with error MSB4057 if a tag existed under in the csproj file. Scans now complete successfully in this scenario.

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where Gradle dependencies failed to resolve for React Native Android projects, ensuring accurate detection and resolution of dependencies by properly handling project structure and Gradle wrapper logic.

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where post-scan cleanup and statistics were not executed if the scan process ended with an error, ensuring proper handling and reporting even when scans fail.

item.title: Mend for GitLab: Version 26.3.1.1 (31-March-2026)
item.description:
Resolved Issues

  • (SAST) Fixed an issue where SAST scan job queues would build up for repositories with large file counts, potentially leading to reduced performance.

item.title: Mend for GitHub Enterprise: Version 26.3.1 (23-March-2026)
item.description:
Unified Agent 26.2.2 | Renovate 43.59.4 | Remediate 26.3.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • (SCA) Added support for the uv package manager, enabling security scanning and Reachability analysis for Python projects managed with uv. Refer to this table for more details.

    • Note: The SCA orchestrator scanner environment variable must be enabled for uv detection to work (MEND_SCA_ORCHESTRATOR_ENABLED=true).

  • Manual scan triggering based on role-based authentication is now supported.

    • The previous method of storing a secret using MEND_CONTROLLER_API_SECRET is still supported.

Resolved Issues

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where scans of .NET projects would fail with error MSB4057 if a tag existed under in the csproj file. Scans now complete successfully in this scenario.

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where Gradle dependencies failed to resolve for React Native Android projects, ensuring accurate detection and resolution of dependencies by properly handling project structure and Gradle wrapper logic.

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where post-scan cleanup and statistics were not executed if the scan process ended with an error, ensuring proper handling and reporting even when scans fail.

item.title: Mend for GitHub.com: Version 26.3.2 (12-April-2026)
item.description:
New Features and Updates

  • (SCA) Introduced a lower priority queue for Reachability "zero day" scans. This enhancement prevents system overload and ensures more reliable scan processing for enterprise customers with large deployments during periods of high CVE alert activity.

item.title: Mend Developer Platform: Version 26.3.2 (12-April-2026)
item.description:
New Features and Updates

  • (SCA) Added support for disabling specific package manager resolvers using the MEND_SCA_{PACAKGE_MANAGER}_RESOLVEDEPENDENCIES environment variable that can be configured in the Credentials page, allowing more granular control over dependency resolution.

item.title: Mend Unified Agent: Version 26.3.2 (14-April-2026)
item.description:
Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where the Unified Agent failed to detect npm dependencies in Yarn Berry (v4) projects using workspaces. The scanner now correctly identifies and processes workspace package.json files and root yarn.lock files, ensuring all dependencies are accurately reported in scan results.

  • Fixed an issue where configuring python.pipPath to an absolute path caused setup.py dependency resolution to detect only one dependency instead of the full dependency tree. All dependencies are now correctly identified during scans when using an absolute pip path.

item.title: Mend CLI: Version 26.3.2 (13-April-2026)
item.description:
New Features and Updates

  • The CLI login process now prompts users to select their organization, ensuring accurate project association and simplifying organization switching between runs.

item.title: Mend AI: Version 25.12.1 (05-January-2026)
item.description:
New Features and Updates

  • (Premium / Core) Introducing System Prompt Risk (open beta), a new detection and remediation offering by Mend AI, for mitigating risks posed by system prompts used in conversational AI interfaces.

    • A new System Prompt Risk table inventories system prompts and provides quick export/sharing and deep-link side panel for prompt context.

    • System Prompt Risks are integrated into the existing AI Security Risk Factors across Projects and Applications. The classification appears as a Conversational Interface chip, is filterable, and automatically participates in dashboards and workflows.

    • Remediation in the form of a hardened system prompt is available in the System Prompt side panel, providing clear, copy-ready remediation guidance for AppSec engineers and developers.

    • The AI Security Dashboard has been enriched with system prompt risk data.

    • System prompt risk data is also available via API.

item.title: Mend Container: Version 26.3.2 (12-April-2026)
item.description:
Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where Azure Container Registry (ACR) integrations would intermittently fail with 401 Unauthorized errors after successful credential validation and initial scans, preventing subsequent scans from running as expected.

item.title: Mend SAST: Version 26.3.2 (13-April-2026)
item.description:
New Features and Updates

  • Improved the formatting of suppression request comments. Comments are now displayed more concisely, with clearer attribution and support for bold and italic text, making it easier to communicate during the review and approval process.

  • Minor accuracy improvements of the remediation suggestions across all supported languages and CWEs.

  • Improved accuracy for CWE-79: Cross-site Scripting detection in React code.

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where the OWASP Top 10 bar chart was missing from the PDF version of the code compliance report.

  • Fixed an issue where the C++ engine reported malformed data flows, causing sink lines to appear in temporary files and preventing snippet reading or signature computation.

  • Fixed an issue related to the MEND_SAST_STORAGE_LIMIT environment variable.

item.title: Mend SCA: Version 26.3.2 (12-April-2026)
item.description:
New Features and Updates

  • Introducing new Zero-Day response workflows in the Mend AppSec Platform, offering immediate risk visibility, audit-level transparency, and user-controlled enforcement for critical vulnerabilities. Users benefit from prominent alerts, a dedicated Zero-Day catalog, configurable workflows, and permanent violation tracking—streamlining investigation and remediation without added operational burden.

  • Improved license accuracy of Maven libraries. Updates include clearer license version specificity, removal of redundant entries, and detection of previously unknown licenses.

item.title: Mend AppSec Platform: Version 26.3.2 (12-April-2026)
item.description:
New Features and Updates

  • Introducing new Zero-Day response workflows in the Mend AppSec Platform, offering immediate risk visibility, audit-level transparency, and user-controlled enforcement for critical vulnerabilities. Users benefit from prominent alerts, a dedicated Zero-Day catalog, configurable workflows, and permanent violation tracking—streamlining investigation and remediation without added operational burden.

  • Enhanced Jira security tickets now provide direct links to relevant areas in the Mend AppSec Platform, display project-level violation details, show violation counts per vulnerability, and make CVE identifiers clickable for quick investigation. These updates give security and AppSec teams richer context and faster access to remediation tools, streamlining the triage and resolution process.

  • UI Improvements:

    • The toggle button group component has been redesigned with a modernized look. Border and divider visibility have been improved across all states, ensuring selected button fills no longer obscure borders and dividers remain clearly visible between buttons.

    • Updated the bulk actions in the My Integrations and Containers Security pages to use a new floating bulk action toolbar, improving clarity of available bulk actions for selected assets.

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where typing at a certain speed in the Applications or Projects search box could cause letters to be removed from the search phrase. The search input now consistently retains all characters as typed.

  • Fixed an issue where Organization Member users could not view data in the "Proprietary" section of the Applications page, instead seeing an "ERR" row. Data now displays correctly in this view.
